section_title: Declaration of solvency.--(1) Where it is proposed to wind up a company voluntarily, the directors of the company
content:
290. Declaration of solvency.--(1) Where it is proposed to wind up a company voluntarily, the directors of the company
or, in the case of a company having more than two directors, the majority of the directors shall at a meeting of the directors
held before the date on which the notice of the meeting at which the resolution of the winding up the company is to be
proposed are sent out, make a declaration verified by an affidavit to the effect that they have made a full inquiry into the
affairs of the company and that, having so done, they formed the opinion that the company will be able to pay its debts in
full within a period, not exceeding three years, from three commencement of the winding up.
(2) Such declaration shall be supported by a report of the company's auditors on the company's affairs, and shall have no
effect for the purposes of this Act unless it is delivered to the Registrar for registration before the date mentioned in sub-
section(1).
(3) A winding up, in the case or which a declaration has been made and delivered in accordance with sub--sections (1) and
(2), is in this Act referred to as "members voluntars winding up", and, where a declaration has not been made and delivered
as aforesaid, is in this Act referred to as "creditors voluntary winding up."
Members' Voluntary Winding up

sub_sections.text:
Where it is proposed to wind up a company voluntarily, the directors of the company
or, in the case of a company having more than two directors, the majority of the directors shall at a meeting of the directors
held before the date on which the notice of the meeting at which the resolution of the winding up the company is to be
proposed are sent out, make a declaration verified by an affidavit to the effect that they have made a full inquiry into the
affairs of the company and that, having so done, they formed the opinion that the company will be able to pay its debts in
full within a period, not exceeding three years, from three commencement of the winding up.

Such declaration shall be supported by a report of the company's auditors on the company's affairs, and shall have no
effect for the purposes of this Act unless it is delivered to the Registrar for registration before the date mentioned in sub-
section(1).

A winding up, in the case or which a declaration has been made and delivered in accordance with sub--sections (1) and
(2), is in this Act referred to as "members voluntars winding up", and, where a declaration has not been made and delivered
as aforesaid, is in this Act referred to as "creditors voluntary winding up."
Members' Voluntary Winding up
